data.title: BIOMAKERS secures $8M funding to expand precision oncology platform, speeding up drug and diagnostic progress.
data.description: In San Francisco, BIOMAKERS has secured an extra $8 million in funding to expand its AI-driven precision oncology platform. This investment will aid in speeding up the progress of drug and diagnostic creation for the company.

data.title: Dalio cautions about banks ditching fiat, praises gold surge. Central banks' divergence in handling fiat could lead to global monetary disorder.
data.description: The hedge fund manager expressed concerns about central banks treating fiat currency differently, highlighting the risk of a possible disruption in the global monetary system.

data.title: Akumin Inc. starts exchange offer, aiming for "qualified institutional buyers" under Rule 144A in the U.S. securities act.
data.description: The information provided is restricted from being released, published, or distributed in the United States, except to Qualified Institutional Buyers (QIBs) under Rule 144A of the U.S. Securities Act of 1933.

data.title: Anaergia S.r.l. secures C$25 million for projects in Spain with D.B. Andalucia. Expanding renewable sector.
data.description: Anaergia Inc. has announced that its subsidiary, Anaergia S.r.l., has secured project contracts for Andujar and Arjona with D.B. Andalucia, a company focused on renewable energy projects in Spain. This agreement is a part of a larger deal revealed on August 19, 2025. Anaergia S.r.l. will provide a wide range of services as per the contract. Anaergia Inc. is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ANRG and on the OTCQX under ANRGF.

data.title: AstraZeneca to delist ADRs from Nasdaq, eyes NYSE listing by January 30, 2026 for broader investor reach.
data.description: AstraZeneca Plc (AZN) revealed plans to delist its American Depositary Shares and specific guaranteed debt securities from the Nasdaq in favor of a direct stock listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE). This transition will see ordinary shares and impacted debt securities commencing trading on the NYSE after the market closes on January 30, 2026, with trading activities anticipated to begin on February 2, 2026. The current Nasdaq listings are slated to end on January 30. This strategic decision aligns with AstraZeneca's shareholder-approved initiative to unify its listing structure globally.

data.title: 77% of Maryland voters want retail energy choice, according to a RESA survey of 808 registered voters in the state.
data.description: In Harrisburg, Pennsylvania, the Retail Energy Supply Association (RESA) disclosed recent results from a Maryland-wide survey carried out by Gonzales Research & Media Services. The data revealed a growing concern among voters regarding escalating energy expenses and a resounding backing for initiatives safeguarding retail energy competition and consumer freedom in the sector. The survey, encompassing 808 registered Maryland voters, was conducted from December 21, 2025, to January 6, 2026. Maryland voters demonstrated significant support for maintaining a competitive marketplace for energy services.

data.title: Vannadium finalist in SXSW Pitch 2026 for AI trust platform in Smart Data, Security, FinTech category.
data.description: Vannadium, a company based in Arlington, Virginia, has been chosen as one of the finalists for SXSW Pitch 2026. They are competing in the Smart Data, Security, and FinTech category with their AI trust infrastructure platform. This platform is designed to enhance security and efficiency in data management using artificial intelligence technology. The recognition highlights Vannadium's innovative approach in this field.

data.title: Advita Ortho appoints Christopher Lin as Chief Legal Officer and Christine Thomas, RAC, as Chief Quality, Regulatory and Clinical Officer.
data.description: Advita Ortho, a prominent player in the global medical technology industry, recently announced key additions to its leadership team to enhance its global expansion and drive innovation. Christopher Lin has been appointed as the Chief Legal Officer, while Christine Thomas, RAC, will take on the role of Chief Quality, Regulatory, and Clinical Officer. These strategic appointments signal the company's commitment to strengthening its legal and regulatory functions. The new hires bring significant experience to the table, empowering Advita Ortho to navigate the complex global landscape effectively. Such moves are crucial as the company pushes forward in its growth trajectory.

data.title: US Bancorp exceeds Q4 expectations, 2026 guidance aligns with estimates; Earnings at $1.26/share, core earnings at $1.28/share.
data.description: US Bancorp (NYSE: USB) exceeded expectations in the fourth quarter with better-than-expected revenues, expenses, credit performance, and earnings. Goldman Sachs analyst Richard Ramsden, in his analysis, maintained a Neutral rating and set a price target of $54 for USB Bancorp. Doing business as U.S. Bank, UB Bancorp provided guidance for 2026 that was mostly in line with expectations. The company's earnings of $1.26 per share and core earnings of $1.28 per share outperformed the consensus of $1.23 per share. Additionally, core PPNR was 2% higher than estimated, primarily due to a 0.5% increase. For more detailed information, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Sidetrade reports 14% revenue growth in 2025, with ACV of €3.25m in Q4 2024. SaaS subscriptions saw 20% growth.
data.description: The fourth quarter of 2024 saw a strong performance in bookings, reaching an Annual Contract Value (ACV) of €3.25 million. Despite a weaker first half of 2025 with a decrease of 21% compared to the record highs of the first half of 2024, there has been a gradual recovery in bookings momentum. In the second half of 2024, ACV remained nearly stable with only a 2.8% decrease. Moving forward into 2025, there has been notable growth in revenue with a 14% increase at constant currency rates. This growth includes a significant rise of 20% in Software as a Service (SaaS) subscriptions. On a reported basis, there was a 12% and 18% increase respectively in SaaS subscriptions, showcasing a strong performance in this sector.

data.title: Tech repair franchise uBreakiFix opens Tucson East store at Park Place, offering fast fixes for phones, tablets, laptops.
data.description: A recently opened store in Tucson, Arizona, called uBreakiFix Tucson East, is the newest franchise location of the leading tech repair provider, uBreakiFix by Asurion. The store, situated at Park Place on 5350 E. Broadway Blvd., Suite 156, offers quick fixes for phones, tablets, laptops, and more. This new location aims to provide quality tech repair services to the community.

data.title: Private investment firm Highlander Partners acquired Tapatio hot sauce, the #5 brand in the US.
data.description: On January 20, 2026, Highlander Partners, L.P., a private investment company located in Dallas, revealed the purchase of Tapatio, recognized as the fifth largest hot sauce brand in the United States. Tapatio holds a strong position in the market due to its authentic appeal that attracts a wide range of consumers, spanning across mainstream and niche markets.

data.title: Lucky Dog Mobile Groomers offers stress-free grooming to pet owners in Houston amid winter weather.
data.description: With the arrival of winter weather in Houston, Lucky Dog Mobile Groomers is offering convenient grooming services delivered directly to pet owners' homes. Amidst dropping temperatures and unstable weather, the company aims to alleviate stress for pet owners. The service began on January 20, 2026, and provides grooming services at the doorstep of pet owners.
